body.title: Class-shim leases reusable instance numbers over a per-class free-list (live object + PID fallback)
body.intention: I intend that a class-shim assigns each instance a reusable number from a per-class free-list, so instance_name numbers reuse freed slots and never collide.
body.description:
**Blocked on: T-shim-frontdoor-on-groundloop** (the shim must be the persistent front-door that holds the free-list). Wires the leasing D-worker-instance-identity deferred.

**What to build:** a per-class free-list held in the class-shim: slot INDEX = instance number, slot VALUE = the live instance object (self), with a PID persisted per instance for crash-recovery.
- ALLOCATE: return the first index whose slot is None; else append a new slot. Caller passes self -> stored at that index (no collision).
- RELEASE: shim sets the slot to None; if it was the LAST slot, trim it.
- REBUILD after a front-door restart: reconstruct from persisted PIDs, liveness-checking each (dead PID = free slot).
- DATA DIRS: the leases file + class-shim state live in ~/.unseen_university/devices/<CLASSNAME>/ (full name, e.g. devices/DickSimnel/); each instance's state+logging lives in ~/.unseen_university/devices/<ABBREV>.<NUMBER>/ (e.g. devices/DS.0/).
- The instance asks the shim for its number during __init__ (D-worker-instance-identity); instance_number stops defaulting to 0 and becomes the leased value.

**Completion criteria:** allocating 3 instances yields DS.0/DS.1/DS.2; releasing DS.1 then allocating again REUSES slot 1 (DS.1); releasing the tail trims the list; a rebuild from PID files after dropping the in-memory list reconstructs the same taken/free set.

**Test plan (real red->green):** unit — drive allocate/release/allocate on a class-shim, assert reuse of the freed index and tail-trim; simulate a front-door restart (drop in-memory list, keep PID files) and assert rebuild matches liveness. Red before the free-list exists; green after.

**Validation (runtime):** devices/DickSimnel/ holds the leases file; a second live DS instance logs under devices/DS.1/; instance_name stays stable across a wake/sleep cycle. Observable via the leases file contents + per-instance folders.

**Logging:** log.info('lease: assigned %s to <class>' , instance_name) on allocate; log.info('lease: released %s', instance_name) on release, at the shim boundary.

**Observable via:** log.info('lease: assigned DS.1') + the appearance of ~/.unseen_university/devices/DS.1/.

**Affected files (indicative):** unseen_university/devices/<worker>/shim.py + BaseShim (the free-list), unseen_university/capabilities/identity.py (instance_number from lease), the class-shim data dir.

**Rollback:** git revert — falls back to instance_number defaulting to 0 (single foreground). No data migration.

**audit-emphasis:** needs-deep-smell (concurrency on the free-list; the number-leak the consequence ticket watches).

**Scope boundary:** the leasing free-list + data dirs + instance_number-from-lease. Pool cap/wipe (T-worker-pool-semantics) and idle-sleep (T-worker-idle-sleep-bus-ask) are separate.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

---

**BUILD SPEC (resolved 2026-07-02, advisor-reviewed — pool lives in the FRONT-DOOR, not BaseShim).** The decision's "class-shim" IS the front-door (the persistent per-class supervisor); BaseShim runs INSIDE each spawned device subprocess (wrong process to hold a class-level free-list). The decision's "slot VALUE = live instance object (self)" was in-process language from before the subprocess model; reconciled: **slot = Popen handle + persisted PID** (the same decision already says "rebuild by liveness-checking persisted PIDs", which only makes sense with a handle+PID). Allocate-before-spawn; number passed to the subprocess via env `UU_INSTANCE_NUMBER`; IdentityMixin reads it.

**File 1 (NEW) `unseen_university/devices/pool.py` — reusable `InstancePool`:** per-class free-list. `__init__(self, class_name, *, liveness=None, home=None)` — `liveness` is an INJECTABLE `Callable[[int, float|None], bool]` (pid, create_time) → alive, defaulting to the real check (below); `class_name` is the FULL name (e.g. "DickSimnel"). State: `self._slots: list[dict|None]` where a live slot = `{"pid": int, "create_time": float|None, "handle": Popen|None}` (handle not persisted). Leases file: `uu_home()/devices/<class_name>/leases.json` (mkdir parents). API:
- `first_free() -> int` — index of first `None` slot, else `len(self._slots)` (append index). Non-mutating peek.
- `allocate(pid, create_time=None, handle=None) -> int` — store the slot at `first_free()`'s index (append if needed), persist leases.json, return the index. (Under the front-door's lock, `first_free()` then `allocate()` are atomic — same index.)
- `release(n) -> None` — set slot n to `None`; if it is the LAST slot, trim ALL trailing `None`s; persist.
- `rebuild() -> None` — load leases.json; for each slot, call `liveness(pid, create_time)`; dead → `None`; trim trailing `None`s; persist. (handle is None after rebuild — pid drives liveness.)
- `taken() -> list[int]` — indices of non-None slots.
- Log INFO at the shim boundary: `lease: assigned <class>.<n>` on allocate, `lease: released <class>.<n>` on release.

**Default liveness (defeats the PID-reuse number-leak — audit-emphasis):** `pid` alive via `os.kill(pid, 0)` (ProcessLookupError→dead, PermissionError→alive) AND, if `create_time` was recorded, it matches `psutil.Process(pid).create_time()` (lazy `import psutil`, as `devices/sensor/device.py` does). If psutil is unavailable, fall back to os.kill-only and `log.warning` once that PID-reuse false-positives are possible (accepted risk: a wrongly-kept slot leaks a number, not a crash; DS is off until free inference). Record `create_time` at allocate time the same way.

**File 2 (MODIFY) `unseen_university/devices/dicksimnel/frontdoor.py` — wire the pool (slot-0 path only):** replace the single `self._proc` with `self._pool = InstancePool("DickSimnel")`; call `self._pool.rebuild()` in `start()` (before the loop). `_device_alive()` → `bool(self._pool.taken())` (any live instance). `_ensure_device_awake()`: under the lock, if `self._pool.taken()`: return; else `n = self._pool.first_free()`, spawn with env `{**os.environ, "UU_FRONTDOOR": "1", "UU_INSTANCE_NUMBER": str(n)}`, `mkdir` the instance dir `uu_home()/devices/DS.<n>/`, then `self._pool.allocate(pid=proc.pid, create_time=<psutil create_time or None>, handle=proc)`. Keep every other front-door behavior (single consumer, idle_wait, logging) unchanged.

**File 3 (MODIFY) `unseen_university/capabilities/identity.py`:** change `instance_number` from a class attr to a **property** returning `int(os.environ.get("UU_INSTANCE_NUMBER", "0"))`. Add `import os`. Do NOT add an `__init__` (MRO-transparency is a hard constraint from D-agent-capability-mixins). Default 0 (no env) preserves DS.0 for standalone/foreground — the existing identity tests (assert instance_name == "DS.0") must stay green.

**PROOF (gates close — stub-first, discriminating node): `tests/test_instance_pool.py::test_freed_slot_is_reused_not_appended`.** allocate three (fake pids) → assert indices 0,1,2; `release(1)`; allocate again → assert returns **1** (the reused freed slot), NOT 3. This FAILS a hollow monotonic-append build (returns 3). Stub `allocate` to always-append (`self._slots.append(slot); return len-1`) → reuse test RED (returns 3); real first-free scan → GREEN. Also unit-test (same file, non-proof): tail-trim on releasing the last slot; `rebuild()` with an injected liveness marking one pid dead reconstructs the correct taken/free set. All use INJECTED liveness + fake pids — NO real subprocesses.

**Scope boundary — two things that look in-scope but are DOWNSTREAM (name, don't solve):** (1) MULTI-SPAWN POLICY (when to spawn a 2nd instance / max_instances cap / folder-WIPE of .1+) is T-worker-pool-semantics — the validation "a second live DS.1 appears" is really that ticket; here the front-door only ever runs slot 0. (2) PER-INSTANCE MAILBOX routing — the front-door idle_waits `dicksimnel.0`; if DS.1 ever spawned, both would consume the same mailbox (the contention the front-door just dissolved). Slot 0 stays `dicksimnel.0` here; per-instance mailbox routing is downstream (T-granny-wake-not-launch). Do NOT build a `BaseFrontDoor` abstraction (one front-door exists — YAGNI).

**Operational:** re-run the front-door wake proof (`tests/test_dicksimnel_frontdoor.py`) after wiring — allocate-before-spawn must NOT regress "spawn only when down."

**Affected files (concrete):** NEW `unseen_university/devices/pool.py`, NEW `tests/test_instance_pool.py`; MODIFY `unseen_university/devices/dicksimnel/frontdoor.py`, `unseen_university/capabilities/identity.py`.
body.result: Per-class instance leasing shipped: reusable InstancePool free-list (slot idx = instance number, slot = pid+create_time+Popen handle) composed into the front-door. allocate() reuses the first freed slot (not monotonic-append), release() clears+tail-trims, rebuild() reconstructs from leases.json culling dead PIDs via injectable liveness (default os.kill + psutil create_time to defeat PID-reuse; warn-once fallback). Front-door allocates-before-spawn, passes UU_INSTANCE_NUMBER via env, mkdir devices/DS.<n>/; IdentityMixin.instance_number is an env-property (__init__-free). Proven: freed slot reused not leaked (reuse red under monotonic-append stub, green with first-free). Scope-held: multi-spawn/max_instances/wipe = pool-semantics; per-instance mailbox = granny-wake.
